text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared main branch. Each time code is added, an automated build is triggered. This build creates a fresh version of the software. After the build, the CI system automatically runs tests to check for errors. The main goal is catching issues early, before they become bigger problems deeper in the development process. These steps form a CI pipeline, which ensures that new code does not break existing functionality. By using CI, teams can work faster and more confidently, knowing their code is being tested every time.
